description:
## Change

In `spec_registry.rs`, when subsumption finds no covering spec for a reachable
FnId, return the any-key spec instead of `None`. Resolve becomes total over
reachable FnIds.

```rust
// after the existing minimal-subsumption pick:
covers.into_iter().min_by_key(|s| s.0)
  .or_else(|| self.any_key(fn_id))   // ← NEW: floor
```

`any_key` already exists as a test helper at the bottom of the file — promote
to public.

## Why this is sound

After fz-aiz.3 lands, every reachable FnId has an any-key spec registered.
The any-key spec's compiled body accepts any Descrs (because its key is
`[any; arity]`), so dispatching there is always type-safe — narrower queries
trivially satisfy `query[i] ⊆ any` for every slot.

## Tests

- Unit: `resolve(fn_id, [any, any])` returns the any-key when no narrower spec covers.
- Unit: when both a narrowed spec and the any-key cover, the narrowed one wins (existing minimal-subsumption logic).
- Unit: `resolve` never returns `None` for a FnId with an any-key.

## Blocked by fz-aiz.3. Blocks fz-aiz.5.

comments.text:
Subsumed by fz-aiz.3e: with `ensure_any_keys` total over every reachable
FnId, the any-key (key = [any; n]) always covers any query via
subsumption (`q ⊆ any` is true for all q). `SpecRegistry::resolve`
therefore returns Some for every reachable FnId without code changes
to spec_registry.rs — the policy change in the typer (the floor)
makes resolve total by construction.

Likewise the .29.x panics in codegen were replaced by .expect() against
the annotation invariant in fz-aiz.3c; no runtime fallback emission is
needed because codegen no longer calls resolve at dispatch sites.

title: fz-aiz.4: SpecRegistry::resolve falls back to any-key
